name: Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica – Circular Route
name_original: Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica –  Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Cugat Del Vallès offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, which borders the municipality. This area features extensive wooded trails, open sunny paths, and varied topography with hills and valleys. Natural features like the Riera de Vallvidrera stream and various fountains are present along many routes. The region provides a mix of terrain suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Sant Cugat Del Vallès The most popular hiking route is Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica – Circular Route, an easy 3.1 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This circular path includes the Ermita Sant Medir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Sant Cugat Centre, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This route forms a loop around significant landmarks like the Pi d'en Xandri and the Sant Medir Hermitage. Local hikers also love the Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Adjutori Hermitage loop from Sant Cugat del Vallès, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through wooded areas and past historical hermitages, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is defined by extensive wooded trails, varied hills, and streams within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Cugat Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: The hiking trails around Sant Cugat Del Vallès primarily traverse the diverse landscapes of the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. You can expect a mix of extensive wooded trails, providing ample shade, alongside open, sunny paths. The topography includes varied hills and valleys, offering a range of experiences from gentle strolls to more challenging ascents. Natural features like the Riera de Vallvidrera stream and various fountains are also present along many routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Yes, there are many options suitable for families. The region offers a good selection of easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943752151/turo-de-magarola-ermita-sant-medir-fuente-vista-rica-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.1 km) circular path that takes about 1 hour 36 minutes to complete, making it a great choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in this region?
A: The Serra de Collserola Natural Park, which encompasses much of the hiking area around Sant Cugat Del Vallès, is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers, and to check specific trail regulations for any restrictions. The varied terrain and wooded paths offer a great environment for walks with your canine companion.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature of hiking in Sant Cugat Del Vallès. Many trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943752151/turo-de-magarola-ermita-sant-medir-fuente-vista-rica-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14909818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Sant Cugat Centre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking routes often lead to or pass by significant historical and natural landmarks. You can visit the popular &lt;strong&gt;Ermita Sant Medir&lt;/strong&gt; (Sant Medir Hermitage), which is often incorporated into hiking loops. Another notable natural landmark is the 250-year-old tree, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt;, which features in several routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14907261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri – Masia de Can Borrell loop from Sant Cugat del Vallès&lt;/a&gt;. Historic farmhouses like Can Borrell and Can Jané also dot the landscape.

Q: How can I access the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès is well-connected, and many trailheads are accessible via public transport, particularly from the town center. The region's proximity to Barcelona and its integration with the Serra de Collserola Natural Park mean that local train (FGC) and bus services can often get you close to starting points. For specific routes, it's best to check local transport schedules to plan your access.

Q: Are there options for easy or beginner-friendly hikes?
A: Absolutely. Sant Cugat Del Vallès offers a substantial number of easy hikes, with nearly 100 routes categorized as easy. These trails are perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943752151/turo-de-magarola-ermita-sant-medir-fuente-vista-rica-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turo de Magarola, Ermita Sant Medir, Fuente Vista Rica – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.1-mile (5.1 km) path.

Q: What about longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For those seeking more challenging experiences, Sant Cugat Del Vallès also offers longer and more difficult routes. The Serra de Collserola Natural Park features diverse topography with hills and valleys that provide opportunities for significant elevation gain. An example of a more challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Adjutori Hermitage loop from Sant Cugat del Vallès&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.8-mile (15.7 km) trail with over 400 meters of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès is suitable for hiking year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, especially on more exposed paths. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and often clear skies, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views?
A: Yes, many trails within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park offer spectacular vistas. You can find panoramic views of the city of Barcelona, Mount Tibidabo, and even the distant Montserrat mountain range from various points along the routes. The park's hills provide natural vantage points for these expansive views. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramar Gardens&lt;/a&gt; for excellent perspectives.

Q: Where can I find parking near the trailheads?
A: Given Sant Cugat Del Vallès's role as a gateway to the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, there are often designated parking areas near popular trailheads, particularly on the outskirts of the town and at key access points to the park. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting point details on komoot or local park information for available parking facilities.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: The hiking trails in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the quiet woodlands, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the Serra de Collserola Natural Park a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural features like streams or natural reserves to look out for?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural features. You can explore areas around the &lt;strong&gt;Riera de Vallvidrera&lt;/strong&gt; (Vallvidrera Stream), which adds to the scenic beauty of many routes. Within Collserola, there are also partial natural reserves like Font Groga and Rierada-Can Balasc, highlighting the area's biodiversity. These spots offer a chance to experience the park's untouched natural environment.
tips: Peak of 430 meters included in the challenge of the 100 peaks of the FEEC. It is a magnificent viewpoint of Barcelona and the Vallès.

Just in front of the Ermita de Sant Medir, on the other side of the esplanade, is the Font del Camp del Miracle. Made by APAC and the Penya Regalèssia, the fountain is presided over by a mosaic in homage to Sant Medir and the gathering that is held here every year at the beginning of March. Built of stoneware, it has four taps with a push button and a large running sink.

The hermitage of San Medín is a sanctuary located in the Collserola mountain range, in the municipality of San Cugat del Vallés (Catalonia). Every March 3, the traditional meeting of San Medin is held there.[1] It is near the old Roman road from Egara (Tarrasa) to Barcino (Barcelona), which passing through Castrum Octavianum (Sant Cugat) entered the Collserola mountain range. through the Gausac valley (or Sant Medir valley).
Formerly called San Emeterio, the hermitage is of Romanesque origin, although due to the transformations it has undergone there are hardly any original traces left. It has a rectangular floor plan with a sacristy. Outside there is a bell tower and the entrance door.

Emblematic place of the Collserola Park and Nature Reserve. The fountain flows from a spout in the center of a wall with a symmetrical structure and a semicircular line. Possibly from the second half of the 19th century. The name comes from the ferruginous content of the water that flows.
A good place from which to start numerous excursions to the park. It has a good parking lot and a public fountain.
